description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the DataForSEO connector arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. This is a read-only, live-only SEO/AI-search data connector, so scenarios are act-only (a single data query against explicit values) or discover-act (a locations/languages lookup resolves the exact locale name before the query); there is nothing to create, so no arrange-act probes and no fixtures. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency/shape diagnostics, our-arm-only). Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

evals.description: Pull the live Google organic SERP for a query and report who ranks.

steps.intent: Who currently ranks on Google in the United States for the search 'best running shoes'? List the top organic results with their URLs.

outcome: The reply lists ranked organic Google results for 'best running shoes', each with a URL/domain and its position, drawn from a live query rather than the model's own recollection.
The results are reported as the current live SERP, not as a historical or made-up ranking.
mechanism: Retrieves the SERP with a single live organic-SERP query for the exact keyword 'best running shoes'; the ranked URLs come from the query result, not fabricated.
Passes the location as the full name 'United States', not an ISO code or abbreviation like 'US'.

evals.description: Expand a seed keyword into related/long-tail query ideas.

steps.intent: Give me the top 5 long-tail keyword ideas related to 'standing desk' that I could target for SEO.

outcome: The reply returns multiple keyword suggestions that are related to or contain the seed 'standing desk', sourced from a live query rather than invented by the model.
The suggestions are concrete keyword strings a user could target, not a generic explanation of how keyword research works.
mechanism: Uses a keyword-expansion query seeded with 'standing desk'; the returned keywords come from the tool result rather than being enumerated from the model's own knowledge.

evals.description: Pull volume / CPC / competition / difficulty for a known keyword list.

steps.intent: Pull the search volume, CPC, and competition for these keywords in the US: 'crm software', 'project management tool', 'time tracking app'.

outcome: The reply reports search volume and CPC (and competition where available) for each of the three requested keywords, from a live query.
Every one of the three keywords given in the request is covered in the reply, none silently dropped.
mechanism: Requests metrics for the three keywords given in the request rather than substituting different keywords.
Passes the location as the full name 'United States', not an ISO code or 'US'.

evals.description: Get keyword-difficulty scores and interpret them on the documented 0-100 scale.

steps.intent: How hard would it be to rank for 'ai writing assistant' versus 'buy shoes online'? Give me a difficulty score for each and tell me which is easier.

outcome: The reply reports a keyword-difficulty score for each of the two keywords and states which is easier to rank for based on those scores.
The difficulty scores are presented on a 0-100 scale (lower = easier), consistent with how keyword difficulty is defined.
mechanism: Obtains the difficulty scores from a keyword-difficulty query for the two keywords given in the request rather than estimating them from the model's own judgment.

evals.description: Classify the search intent of a set of keywords.

steps.intent: Classify the search intent (informational, commercial, transactional, navigational) for these queries: 'how to tie a tie', 'best noise cancelling headphones', 'buy iphone 15'.

outcome: The reply assigns a search-intent classification to each of the three queries given in the request, based on a live query rather than the model guessing.
All three queries from the request are classified, none skipped.
mechanism: Classifies the exact three queries from the request using a search-intent query rather than substituting or reasoning about intent unaided.

evals.description: List the keywords a domain ranks for in Google organic search.

steps.intent: What keywords does the domain 'basecamp.com' rank for in Google in the US? Show me the top 5 ranked keywords.

outcome: The reply lists keywords that basecamp.com ranks for, with ranking detail (e.g. position or the ranking URL), from a live query.
The keywords are reported as basecamp.com's actual ranked keywords from the data source, not the model's guesses about what the site might rank for.
mechanism: Queries ranked keywords for the target domain 'basecamp.com' given in the request; results come from the tool call.
Passes the location as the full name 'United States', not 'US' or a country code.

evals.description: Estimate a domain's current monthly organic traffic.

steps.intent: Roughly how much monthly organic search traffic does 'shopify.com' get? Give me an estimate.

outcome: The reply gives an estimated monthly organic-traffic figure for shopify.com sourced from a live query.
The figure is framed as an estimate, not stated as an exact measured count.
mechanism: Obtains the estimate from an organic-traffic query for the domain 'shopify.com' given in the request rather than fabricating a number.

evals.description: Summarize a target's backlink profile (totals, referring domains, rank).

steps.intent: Give me an overview of the backlink profile for 'basecamp.com' — total backlinks, referring domains, and its backlink rank.

outcome: The reply reports a backlink-profile summary for basecamp.com including total backlinks and referring-domain count, from a live query.
Any backlink-rank value reported is interpreted on its documented 0-1000 scale (higher = stronger), not confused with a 0-100 score.
mechanism: Retrieves the summary for the target 'basecamp.com' given in the request via a backlinks-summary query; the totals come from the result rather than being invented.

evals.description: List the domains linking to a target.

steps.intent: Which domains link to 'basecamp.com'? Show me the top 5 referring domains.

outcome: The reply lists referring domains that link to basecamp.com, sourced from a live query rather than the model's recollection.
The referring domains are concrete domain names from the data, not a generic description of what a backlink is.
mechanism: Queries referring domains for the target 'basecamp.com' given in the request; the domains come from the tool result.

evals.description: Run an instant on-page SEO audit of a single URL.

steps.intent: Run an on-page SEO audit of 'https://example.com' and tell me what on-page issues it has.

outcome: The reply reports on-page SEO findings for the single URL https://example.com (e.g. title/meta, headings, or detected issues) from a live audit query.
The findings are specific to the audited page rather than generic SEO advice.
mechanism: Audits the exact URL 'https://example.com' given in the request as a single page; does not attempt to crawl or audit additional pages of the site.

evals.description: Search Google Maps business listings by category and location.

steps.intent: Find the top 5 coffee shops in Seattle on Google Maps and list them with their ratings.

outcome: The reply lists Google Maps business listings for coffee shops in Seattle, with identifying detail such as name and (where available) rating, from a live query.
The listings are real results from the data source rather than businesses the model recalls or invents.
mechanism: Searches business listings for the coffee-shop category in the Seattle location given in the request; results come from the tool call.

evals.description: Send a prompt to a named AI model via DataForSEO and return its answer.

steps.intent: Ask ChatGPT 'What are three good name ideas for a productivity app?' and show me its answer.

outcome: The reply contains an answer to the prompt that was obtained by querying the requested AI model through the DataForSEO connector, not one the agent composed itself or fetched via a direct call to another AI provider's own API.
The answer addresses the asked question (name ideas for a productivity app).
mechanism: Sends the prompt given in the request to a ChatGPT model via the connector rather than answering the prompt directly from the agent's own generation.

evals.description: Find where a brand/domain is mentioned across AI-generated answers.

steps.intent: Where does the brand 'Notion' get mentioned in AI answers about productivity tools? Show me where it shows up.

outcome: The reply reports where 'Notion' is mentioned in AI-generated answers (e.g. the prompts/answers or pages/domains it appears alongside), from a live query.
The mentions are drawn from the AI-mentions data source rather than the model asserting from its own knowledge where Notion tends to be mentioned.
mechanism: Queries the LLM-mentions surface for the target brand 'Notion' given in the request; findings come from the tool result.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: DataForSEO requires exact full location/language names, so the agent should resolve the exact accepted names before running a locale-scoped query rather than guessing an ISO code.

steps.intent: I want search volume for 'wireless earbuds' for German searchers in Germany. First find the exact location and language names DataForSEO accepts for Germany and German.

outcome: The reply reports the exact full location name for Germany and language name for German that the data source accepts (e.g. 'Germany' and 'German'), obtained from a lookup rather than assumed.
The reply surfaces accepted full-name strings, not ISO codes like 'DE' / 'de'.
mechanism: Resolves the accepted location/language names via the locations-and-languages lookup rather than guessing or hard-coding a country/language code.

steps.intent: Now pull the search volume for 'wireless earbuds' using those German location and language names.

outcome: The reply reports search volume for 'wireless earbuds' scoped to Germany / German, from a live query.
The volume is presented as data for the German locale specifically, not worldwide or US figures.
mechanism: Passes the location and language as the exact full names accepted by the data source (e.g. 'Germany' / 'German'), not ISO codes; the values are the ones resolved earlier in this session, not invented.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: AI keyword search volume is a modeled proxy derived from 'People Also Ask' data, not measured AI-tool usage — the answer should reflect that framing.

steps.intent: How often is the keyword 'chatgpt alternatives' searched inside AI tools? Give me the AI search volume and tell me how reliable that number is.

outcome: The reply reports an AI-keyword search-volume figure for 'chatgpt alternatives' from a live query.
The reply conveys that this figure is an estimate/modeled proxy rather than a directly measured count of real AI-tool usage.
mechanism: Obtains the figure from the AI-keyword-search-volume query for the keyword given in the request rather than fabricating a usage number.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: LLM-mentions ChatGPT-platform data is available only for United States / English, so a request for ChatGPT mentions in another locale should surface that limitation rather than silently returning unrelated data.

steps.intent: Show me where the brand 'Trello' is mentioned in ChatGPT's answers for French users in France.

outcome: The reply makes clear that ChatGPT-platform mention data is limited to the United States / English and is not available for the France / French locale requested, rather than presenting French-locale ChatGPT results as if they were returned.
The reply does not fabricate France-specific ChatGPT mention data.
mechanism: Does not silently pass an unsupported non-US locale to a ChatGPT-platform mentions query and report the output as the requested French data.

evals.description: Report the DataForSEO account credit balance and plan limits.

steps.intent: How much DataForSEO credit do I have left on my account?

outcome: The reply reports the account's current credit balance (and plan/rate detail where available) from a live account query.
The balance figure comes from the account rather than being invented.
mechanism: Retrieves the balance via the account-balance query rather than any data-query tool.

evals.description: Multi-query flow: compare what two competing domains rank for to surface overlap/gaps.

steps.intent: Compare the top 5 ranked keywords each for 'basecamp.com' and 'asana.com' in Google US search and tell me some keywords one ranks for that the other does not.

outcome: The reply reports ranked keywords for both basecamp.com and asana.com and identifies at least some keywords where the two domains differ, based on live queries for each domain.
The comparison is grounded in retrieved ranking data for both domains, not the model's assumptions about either site.
mechanism: Retrieves ranked keywords for both domains given in the request (a query per domain) before comparing; the keyword lists come from the tool results.
Passes the location as the full name 'United States', not 'US'.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector is live-only with no scheduling/monitoring; a request to track rankings over time should be declined cleanly.

steps.intent: Set up daily tracking of my domain 'mystore.com' ranking for 'running shoes' and alert me whenever the position changes.

outcome: The reply explains that ongoing/scheduled ranking tracking and alerting is not supported — each query returns a one-shot live result — rather than claiming monitoring was set up.
The reply does not report that any recurring task, schedule, or alert was created.
mechanism: Does not fabricate a scheduling/monitoring action; at most it takes a single live ranking reading, and does not claim to have created a recurring job.

evals.description: Graceful refusal / scope-correction: the on-page audit covers a single URL only; a full-site crawl request should be declined or scoped down honestly.

steps.intent: Crawl the entire 'example.com' website and give me an SEO audit of every page.

outcome: The reply makes clear that a full-site crawl of every page is not supported and that only a single page can be audited at a time, rather than claiming the whole site was crawled.
The reply does not present fabricated results for many pages as though an entire-site crawl had run.
mechanism: Does not claim to have crawled multiple pages; if it audits anything it audits a single URL and is transparent that coverage is one page.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector is read-only and cannot modify any Google property; a write request should be declined cleanly.

steps.intent: Submit my sitemap at 'https://mystore.com/sitemap.xml' to Google so my pages get indexed faster.

outcome: The reply explains that submitting a sitemap (or any write to a Google property) is not something this connector can do — it only retrieves SEO/search data — rather than claiming the sitemap was submitted.
The reply does not claim any submission, indexing request, or other write action succeeded.
mechanism: Does not call any tool in an attempt to perform the write, and does not report a fabricated success.
